pages.title: College Overview
pages.content:
### Welcome to Trongsa College of Heritage and Contemporary Studies
The **Trongsa College of Heritage and Contemporary Studies**, originally established as the **College of Language and Culture Studies (CLCS)**, is one of the premier constituent colleges of the **Royal University of Bhutan (RUB)**. 

Located in Taktse, overlooking the historic Trongsa Dzong, the cradle of the royal Wangchuck dynasty, our college serves as a vibrant hub of linguistic excellence, cultural preservation, and intellectual advancement.

#### Our Commitment to Excellence
CLCS was founded with the special mandate from His Majesty the King of Bhutan to preserve, cultivate, and promote national language (Dzongkha), Bhutanese cultural heritage, values, and traditions, while equipping young minds with modern contemporary studies to innovate in a globalized world. 

With five distinct bachelor level academic offerings spanning Culture, Psychology, Global Affairs, and Entrepreneurship, we prepare graduates to be mindful leaders, pioneering thinkers, and custodians of GNH (Gross National Happiness).

#### Campus Location
The college is situated at Taktse, around 24 kilometers from Trongsa town on the highway to Trashigang. The majestic mountain air, pristine woodlands, and architectural magnificence of the Bhutanese style campus provide a tranquil and spiritually elevating setting for academic pursuit.

pages.title: Vision, Mission, and Values
pages.content:
### Inspiring Generations, Anchored in Heritage

#### Our Vision
To be a prestigious, world-class institution of higher learning in Bhutanese language, cultural heritage, and contemporary socio-humanities; fostering mindfulness, creative expression, and rigorous scholarship dedicated to GNH.

#### Our Mission
1. **Promote Language & Preservation**: Lead research and educational excellence in Dzongkha, culture, art, religion, and the rich heritage of the Kingdom of Bhutan.
2. **Global Integration**: Engage in multi-disciplinary research, linking Bhutanese intellectual heritage with contemporary sciences, global studies, and sustainable innovations.
3. **Values-Led Leadership**: Graduate compassionate, ethically grounded citizens imbued with the philosophy of Gross National Happiness and mindful responsibility.

#### Core Values
- **Ley Jm-Drey (ལས་རྒྱུ་འབྲས།)**: Integrity and cause-and-effect accountability in actions.
- **Thag-Dam-Tse (མཐུན་ལམ་དམ་ཚིག།)**: Utmost fidelity and harmony within our college community and country.
- **Academic Rigour**: Tireless commitment to scientific inquiry, intellectual inquiry, and critical thinking.
- **Mindfulness (Sampa)**: Cultivating calm awareness and GNH values in everyday academic life.

pages.title: College Historyys
pages.content:
### From Semtokha to Taktse: Our Journey

The history of the College of Language and Culture Studies (Taktse, Trongsa) is deeply intertwined with the educational history of modern Bhutan.

#### Origin as Semtokha Rigzhung
The institution traced its origin back to **Semtokha Rigzhung School**, established in **July 1961** by the third King, His Majesty Jigme Dorji Wangchuck, under the spiritual guidance of the esteemed scholar Dilgo Khyentse Rinpoche. The initial mandate was to teach Dzongkha, classical languages, and traditional philosophies to young Bhutanese.

#### Elevation to College Status
In 1997, Semtokha Rigzhung was converted into the **Rigzhung Institute** under the Ministry of Education. With the birth of the **Royal University of Bhutan** in **2003**, the institute became one of the founding constituent colleges.

#### Relocation to Taktse, Trongsa
To expand Its horizons and provide state-of-the-art facilities, the college relocated from Semtokha (Thimphu) to its present beautiful campus in **Taktse, Trongsa** in **2005**. The tranquil valleys of Trongsa offer the ultimate retreat for classical studies, allowing students to connect intimately with the historic core of the country.

Today, the college has renamed itself **Trongsa College of Heritage and Contemporary Studies**, expanding its spectrum beyond traditional languages into psychology, cultural entrepreneurship, history, and global matters.

pages.title: Principal's Message
pages.content:
### Welcome from the Principal of Trongsa College

> *"Inherited wisdom and pioneering innovations must walk hand in hand. At Trongsa College, we prepare you for a world that requires both technical smartness and spiritual depth."*

Greetings, and a warm Bhutanese **Tashi Delek** to all students, scholars, parents, and visitors!

As the Principal of this illustrious institution, I welcome you to our digital gateway. Nestled under the clear skies of Taktse, Trongsa College represents a unique pedagogical philosophy—we are a sanctuary where Bhutan's legendary language, culture, and cultural arts coalesce with contemporary disciplines like global affairs, entrepreneurship, and mindfulness psychology.

Our college is of strategic national importance. Under the Royal University of Bhutan, we are tasked with shaping the intellectual vanguard who will carry Bhutan's distinct voice into the 21st century. Our faculty members are leading experts in Dzongkha linguistics, cultural heritage preservation, Buddhist studies, and modern social sciences.

Whether you are an aspiring undergraduate looking to join one of our iconic BA programs, a global researcher fascinated by the Bhutan Cultural Atlas, or an alumnus returning to support our student communities, we invite you to join us in our pursuit of knowledge that is rigorous, beautiful, and deeply human.

May peace, joy, and wisdom guide your endeavors.

**Dr. Sonam Tobgay**  
*Principal, Trongsa College of Heritage & Contemporary Studies (CLCS)*

pages.title: Research Centre for Buddhist Studies
pages.content:
### Research Centre for Buddhist Studies

The Research Centre for Buddhist Studies supports academic inquiry into Buddhist philosophy, textual traditions, contemplative practice, and Bhutanese spiritual heritage. The centre provides a scholarly platform for research, seminars, publications, and dialogue between academics, practitioners, and cultural knowledge holders.

#### Current Focus Areas

- Buddhist philosophy, ethics, epistemology, and hermeneutics
- Bhutanese religious history, monastic learning, and textual preservation
- Applied Buddhist perspectives on peace, leadership, ecology, and contemporary society

pages.title: Bhutan & Himalayan Research Centre
pages.content:
### Bhutan & Himalayan Research Centre

The Bhutan & Himalayan Research Centre advances interdisciplinary study of Bhutan and the wider Himalayan region through field research, archival work, cultural documentation, and regional collaboration. It serves as a platform for studying communities, landscapes, languages, heritage, and social change across the Himalayas.

#### Current Focus Areas

- Himalayan cultural heritage, oral traditions, sacred geography, and local histories
- Bhutanese society, language, environment, migration, and contemporary transformation
- Collaborative research with communities, universities, archives, and regional partners

programmes.title: Bachelor of Arts in Language and Heritage Studies
programmes.description: An intensive flagship program focused on the preservation of traditional Bhutanese texts, Dzongkha linguistics, and cultural arts conservation. It equips students with the linguistic aptitude and historical mastery required to act as custodians of Bhutanese heritage.
programmes.eligibility: Class XII passed with a minimum of 50% in Dzongkha, and satisfactory scores in three other main academic subjects. Excellent proficiency in written and spoken Dzongkha is highly desirable.

programmes.title: Bachelor of Arts in Bhutan Studies and Global Perspectives
programmes.description: A hybrid multi-disciplinary degree combining traditional GNH governance philosophies, Bhutanese native history, and contemporary geopolitical dynamics. Imparts deep understanding of Bhutan's role in the modern world context.
programmes.eligibility: Class XII passed with 55% aggregate, including English and four relevant humanities subjects.

programmes.title: Bachelor of Arts in Cultural Innovation and Entrepreneurship
programmes.description: A breakthrough professional program merging traditional crafts, performing arts, and heritage assets with modern digital marketing, startup mechanics, tour-agency structures, and sustainable cultural tourism innovations.
programmes.eligibility: Class XII passed from any stream with 50% aggregate and passing grade in English.

programmes.title: Bachelor of Arts in History and Global Affairs
programmes.description: Explore world civilizations, East-Asian history, international relations, and diplomatic strategies. Students analyze historical shifts while building critical debate and international policy expertise standard in global service roles.
programmes.eligibility: Class XII passed with 55% aggregate in Arts/Humanities streams or equivalent, with strong English writing standards.

programmes.title: Bachelor of Arts in Psychology and Mindfulness Studies
programmes.description: An innovative, globally unique curriculum blending modern cognitive psychology, therapeutic sciences, and neurobiology with ancient Bhutanese mindfulness meditative systems, emotional regulation, and GNH wellness styles.
programmes.eligibility: Class XII passed with a minimum of 50% aggregate score (all streams accepted) with strong competency in written English and personal analytical interview.

staffProfiles.name: Dependra Basnet
staffProfiles.bio: Doctor of Linguistics from the University of London. Serves as current academic overseer of CLCS and advisor to Bhutan Ministry of Education on traditional curricula.

staffProfiles.name: Lopon Jigme Wangdi
staffProfiles.designation: Executive Dean of Dzongkha and Cultural Studies
staffProfiles.bio: Senior Chokay and Dzongkha scriptural scholar. Directs translation audits for historical thangkas and scriptural repositories.

staffProfiles.name: Mrs. Dechen Peldon
staffProfiles.designation: Senior Head of Research & Rigzoed Editorial Board
staffProfiles.bio: PhD researcher specializing in Himalayan cultural adaptation and rural heritage economic development models. Author of numerous Rigzoed publications.

researchPublications.title: Dzongkha Phonology Patterns: A Computational Mapping of Regional Valleys
researchPublications.author: Lopon Jigme Wangdi & Mrs. Dechen Peldon
researchPublications.description: An empirical mapping explaining phonetic variations across the eastern and western dzongkhags, analyzing modern phonetic shift trends among urban school cohorts.

researchPublications.title: Integrative Mindfulness in Contemporary Schools: Appreciating Mind-Relief (Sem-Drey) Traditions
researchPublications.author: Mrs. Dechen Peldon
researchPublications.description: A comprehensive analysis measuring academic productivity increases inside schools adopting morning traditional breathing routines.

studentServices.title: Campus Life & Hostels
studentServices.description: Comfortable residential hostels overlooking the deep gorges of Trongsa, offering wholesome student environments.
studentServices.content:
### Living in the lap of culture
Accommodation is fully boarding with separate modern hostels for male and female undergraduate student cohorts. Standard meals are provided at the central college canteen mess three times daily under hygienic standards managed by the Student Parliament Welfare Comittee. 

Students engage in morning prayers (Rig-Zhey) and evening traditional walks, making dorm life an absolute learning sanctuary.

studentServices.title: Advanced IT Labs
studentServices.description: Fully connected computational hubs running optical fiber links to assist with cultural atlas mappings and coding.
studentServices.content:
### Computational Excellence for Humanistic Arts
We challenge the misconception that culture studies do not need high technology. Inside our dynamic IT lab, we support digital humanities. 

Students use high-spec computers to record dialects, draft digital atlas archives, coordinate GIS mappings, and design websites to support cultural ventures.

studentServices.title: Traditional Sports & Games Center
studentServices.description: Enjoy national Bhutanese archery, Khuru (darts), alongside classic football, volleyball, and modern basketball yards.
studentServices.content:
### Body & Mind Alignment
Aligned with Bhutanese philosophies of healthy communal bonding, Trongsa College maintains prime soccer and basketball yards. Archery, the legendary national sport of Bhutan, is actively integrated during major seasonal college competitions (Tsechus).
